paper_title: Field Sampling of GRIP Ice Core

methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing ice core samples from the GRIP ice core for further chemical and isotopic analysis. This method is used to obtain high-resolution data on past climate conditions through the analysis of stable oxygen isotopes and volcanic signals.

steps.description: The 3029 m GRIP ice core was drilled at the summit of the Greenland ice cap from 1989-1992.
steps.searchable_summary: drill and extract GRIP ice core from Greenland ice cap

steps.description: The 10 cm diameter core was first cut into 55 cm lengths and then sub-sampled with longitudinal cuts for further measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: cut ice core into 55 cm lengths and sub-sample for measurements

steps.description: A continuous ∼ 2 mm resolution ECM acidity profile was created in situ in the GRIP sub-surface laboratories.
steps.searchable_summary: create ECM acidity profile at 2 mm resolution

steps.description: The ECM profile reveals acid peaks ascribed to volcanic signals through the Holocene and constituted the basis for selecting ice core sequences for more detailed chemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ECM profile for volcanic signals

steps.description: More than 100,000 samples were cut in situ using a stainless steel band saw for laboratory analyses of stable oxygen isotopes (δ 18 O).
steps.searchable_summary: cut samples for stable oxygen isotope analysis

steps.description: The seasonal variation of δ 18 O provided the basis of precise stratigraphic dating back to 1800 BC.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ 18 O for stratigraphic dating

steps.description: Major volcanic signals were later used to tie the Holocene GRIP record to the NGRIP and DYE-3 Greenland ice core records, resulting in an estimated dating accuracy of ±2 yr for the 2000 yr old ice strata.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate volcanic signals with NGRIP and DYE-3 records
